_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
There is no EPR-style producer registration scheme under this regulatory framework. The obligations are animal health certification and movement licence requirements administered by three competent authorities: DAERA (Northern Ireland), APHA (Great Britain), and Scottish Government. The workspace must track registration/licence status at the consignment level rather than at an organisational registration level.

**Standard GB export certification (DAERA systems):** Exports of cattle from Northern Ireland to Great Britain require application through the aPVP using eAVI. Exports of sheep and goats use eSAVI. The workspace must ensure an aPVP is engaged per consignment and that the resulting certificate number is captured, as it must be entered into the Scotland supplemental certificate to link the two documents.

**Scotland supplemental certificate (DAERA publication):** This is a fillable Word document downloadable directly from the DAERA publication page. It is not submitted to a registry; it travels physically with the animals. The workspace must maintain version control as DAERA has published separate templates for: (i) Direct Slaughter — cattle; (ii) Direct Slaughter — sheep and goats; (iii) Breeding & Production (March – 31 May) — cattle; (iv) Breeding & Production (March – 31 May) — sheep and goats; (v) Breeding & Production (1 June 2026 onwards) — cattle [currently marked "not yet in use, for info only"]; (vi) Breeding & Production (1 June 2026 onwards) — sheep and goats [currently marked "not yet in use, for info only"]; and (vii) Additional Supplementary Owner's Declaration for cattle.

**Scottish Government movement licences (from 1 June 2026):** From 1 June 2026, all movements of bluetongue-susceptible animals and germinal products from the BTV-3 Restricted Zone to Scotland will require a licence. General licences (including EXD608, which has been updated to cover all animal categories moving from a Restricted Zone to Scotland to live) do not require an individual application — the operator self-declares compliance. The EXD645 pro forma must be completed by the keeper ahead of the move, gathering supporting evidence of PCR/ELISA test results or vaccination records, and endorsed by sales/collection centre operators where applicable. Specific licences (for movements not covered by the general licence) must be individually applied for via APHA, with at least 10 days lead time recommended. General licences are expected to be published by the Scottish Government prior to 1 June 2026; the workspace must monitor gov.scot for publication.

**Data fields the workspace must collect per Scotland-bound consignment:** consignor premises address and disease history (bluetongue confirmed in last 6 months — yes/no); species and purpose of movement (breeding/production or slaughter); standard GB certificate number from DAERA systems; vaccination status and date of completion of primary course (cattle: Bultavo-3; sheep/goats: Syvazul, Bultavo, or Bluevac); PCR test result and sample date (where applicable); insecticide pour-on treatment date (for June–September 2026 consignments); destination premises and, for slaughter, confirmation of designated abattoir status.

*Source: Moving cattle, sheep and goats to Scotland while Scotland is Bluetongue Free, DAERA, 2026. https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/moving-cattle-sheep-and-goats-scotland-while-scotland-bluetongue-free; Movement controls from 1 June 2026, Scottish Government, March 2026. https://www.gov.scot/publications/bluetongue/pages/movement-restrictions-from-1-june-2026/*
